title: Yacht Charter in Maldives: Costs and What to Know 2026
html:
A yacht charter in the Maldives sails turquoise atolls from a departure point such as Hulhumale Marina near Male, with day rates from roughly MVR 9,000 to MVR 70,000 depending on the boat. BoatHire24 lists crewed yachts, catamarans and dhonis across the archipelago for snorkelling and island hopping.

Where to set off in Maldives

Most charters begin near the capital and fan out across the atolls. The Maldives is a chain of roughly 26 natural atolls, so a sensible base keeps crossings short.

  • Hulhumale and Male: the main hub beside Velana International Airport, where the majority of crewed yachts collect guests.
  • North and South Male Atoll: home to famous surf breaks and house reefs near the capital.
  • Baa Atoll: a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve where Hanifaru Bay draws manta rays in season.
  • Ari Atoll: long-favoured for diving, with frequent reef shark and manta encounters.
  • Maafushi: a populated local island handy for budget guesthouse stays and shared trips.

What is the best yacht to charter in the Maldives?

The best yacht for most visitors is a crewed catamaran or motor yacht, as it offers stable decks, shade and the range to reach distant atolls in comfort. Your choice depends on group size, budget and how far you want to roam. The options below cover the boats listed most often.

  1. Crewed catamaran: best for families and stability over long crossings; sleeps 6 to 10 guests; from MVR 28,000 per day.
  2. Motor yacht: best for speed between atolls and easy snorkelling stops; carries 8 to 12 guests; from MVR 35,000 per day. For a planing cruiser of this class, consider this motor yacht as a fast, comfortable base.
  3. Traditional dhoni: best for shorter local trips and snorkelling near house reefs; seats 8 to 12 guests; from MVR 9,000 per half day.
  4. Liveaboard safari boat: best for multi-day diving with full board; sleeps 12 to 20 guests; from MVR 22,000 per person per week.
  5. Sailing monohull: best for quiet, wind-driven cruising on a budget; sleeps 4 to 6 guests; from MVR 24,000 per day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in the Maldives?

Prices vary widely with the boat, the crew and the itinerary. As a rough guide for 2026, the bands below reflect typical day and tour rates.

  • A shared snorkelling or dolphin tour from Maafushi costs from about MVR 800 per person.
  • A small motorboat or dhoni for a half day runs from roughly MVR 9,000.
  • A crewed catamaran for a full day starts near MVR 28,000.
  • A fully crewed luxury yacht can reach MVR 70,000 or more per day in peak season.

You can compare crews, cabins and itineraries and then book a charter online once a boat and date suit your plans. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in the Maldives or list your own.

When is the best season to charter in the Maldives?

The dry north-east monsoon, from December to April, brings the calmest seas and clearest water, making it the high season for charters. The wetter south-west monsoon, known locally as Hulhangu, runs roughly May to November and can bring squalls, though manta sightings in Baa Atoll peak then.

Visit Hanifaru Bay in Baa Atoll between June and November for the manta and whale shark aggregations, and book a guided snorkel slot in advance, as numbers in the bay are capped to protect the rays.

The Maldives operates as an island nation where local crews hold the licences, so most charters come captained and you simply join as a guest. The operator confirms exact qualification and permit requirements for any bareboat option before departure.

At a glance

  • Price band: from MVR 9,000 to MVR 70,000 per day
  • Peak months: December to April
  • Trip length: half day, full day or multi-day liveaboard
  • Capacity: typically 6 to 20 guests
  • Currency: Maldivian rufiyaa (MVR)
  • Nearest hub: Hulhumale, near Velana International Airport

Questions about boat rental in the Maldives

Do I need a licence to charter a yacht in the Maldives?

Most yachts in the Maldives are chartered with a local captain and crew, so guests need no licence at all. The operator confirms the precise requirements for any bareboat arrangement.

How far is it from Male to the outer atolls?

Baa Atoll lies roughly 65 nautical miles north of Male, so reaching it usually means an overnight cruise or a seaplane transfer. Many charters stay within North and South Male Atoll to maximise water time.

Can I see manta rays and whale sharks on a charter?

Yes, manta rays gather in Baa Atoll between June and November, while whale sharks appear year-round around South Ari Atoll. A guided liveaboard gives the best chance of reliable encounters.

How many people fit on a Maldives yacht charter?

Day charters typically carry between 6 and 12 guests, while larger liveaboard safari boats sleep up to 20. Capacity depends on the vessel, so confirm the cabin and deck layout when you enquire.

The Maldives rewards anyone who plans around the December to April calm. Compare boats, crews and atolls, then browse the listings to secure your dates, and boat owners can list a vessel to reach travellers worldwide.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in the Maldives and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
